floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: sleep/debug menu handler (14 DispSleepMenuTex rows + 2-player pad loop + dual 12-case jump-table dec/inc + wrap normalization) -- the crux is the D_8010277C..87 byte-block typing, w

frontier.hypothesis: Joint retype {D_80102784, D_80102785, D_80102787 -> s8} in this TU (plus s8* block pointer for phase-A rows 1/12/13) reproduces every lb in this function natively, while func_800343F0's sibling sites keep byte-identity via (s8)(u8) double value casts -- no pointer-pun coercion anywhere.
frontier.mechanism: type-level instruction selection: s8 decl -> lb (native); (u8) value cast -> combine.c narrows zero_extend-of-load to lbu; outer (s8) on the zero-extended SI value -> explicit sll24/sra24 pair.
frontier.next_probe: Draft the retype + this function's phase A; sandbox func_80034708 AND func_800343F0 (its committed form must stay byte-identical or be re-spelled (s8)(u8) in the same change); grep the TU for every other lbu user of 84/85/87 before committing the retype.

frontier.hypothesis: The dual jump-table switch pair comes from source `switch (cursor)` inside `if (left) ... else if (right) ...` with cases 8/9 (D_80106A73 ^= 1 / ^= 2) written identically in both switches -- GCC's cross-jumping merged the identical xori case bodies across the two switches (both jtbls point at the same case addresses).
frontier.mechanism: jump.c cross_jump pass merges byte-identical tails ending in the same join label .L80034D9C; xori toggle is direction-independent so dec and inc switches share those bodies.
frontier.next_probe: Draft both switches with identical case-8/9 bodies; if the shared addresses do not reproduce, consult cross-jump-call-merge / cross-jump-store-tail-merge rules for the forcing conditions.

frontier.hypothesis: Phase-B walking pointers (s1/s2/s3 from a common s7 anchor, +1 per iteration; s6/s0 +2) come from explicit per-player pointer locals initialized before the loop (p7C=&D_8010277C; p7E=...; p80=...;) and incremented at the tail -- not from array[i] indexing (which would emit base+i addiu per use, not persistent registers).
frontier.mechanism: GCC 2.7.2 keeps address-of-global pointer locals in callee-saved regs across jals; the s7-relative addiu initialization falls out of CSE on the shared %hi(D_80102784) when the inits are adjacent.
frontier.next_probe: Draft with explicit pointer locals + increments; check the s7-anchored init cluster (addiu s1,s7,-8 etc.) reproduces; if the anchor CSE fails, try ordering the pointer inits after taking &D_80102784 first.
